pattern: A run of reversals cancels out, so track only a direction flag and grow the result from whichever end is currently 'front' instead of physically flipping the buffer each time.
hints: Physically reversing a growing string on every trigger character is wasteful - two reversals in a row undo each other, so what actually matters is just parity.
Keep a boolean for whether the string is currently 'flipped'. A normal character always gets appended on the logical-end side that matches the current flip state.

Use a deque and a `flipped` flag: on the trigger character toggle `flipped`; on any other character, push to the back if not flipped, or push to the front if flipped. At the end, reverse once if `flipped` is still true, then join.

edge_inputs.description: one trigger reversing a short prefix
two triggers in a row cancel each other out
trigger character appears at every other position
maximum-length string that is one long run before a single trigger near the end
string ends on the trigger character itself
many consecutive triggers stress the parity toggle, not a literal reversal count

stress.description: length-100 string with trigger characters scattered at random positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = 'abcdefghjklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    chars = [rng.choice(alphabet + 'i') for _ in range(n - 1)]
    first = rng.choice(alphabet)
    s = first + ''.join(chars)
    return [s]
stress.note: first character is forced to be a non-trigger letter, matching the constraint s[0] != 'i'
